summary: Package ships XMRig Monero miner as its main binary with hardcoded author wallet
details: The package's `main` entry is a 24MB ELF binary named `launch` that is the XMRig CPU miner (strings include randomx, cn-heavy, cn-lite, cn-pico, donate-level, and pool/keepalive logic). The shipped `config.json` hardcodes the mining pool `pool.hashvault.pro:80` and the author's Monero wallet `4AUTfJGTvT8A98PSE99EunfrnUbkWe9vU3FN3qsABQLP5aPKQuvWS5sCaD9WaXFQUqNtDLb9dcxiBgJiWCfjvDXp5ptWDYG`. `package.json` declares `"start": "./launch"`, so `npm start` runs the miner directly; the installer's CPU and electricity generate Monero rewards paid to the author. A `deploy` script additionally moves `libuv.so` into `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/lib` and installs `xmr`/`edit` shell shims into `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin`, where the `xmr` shim re-enters `npm start` to launch the miner from any shell prompt. The package has no legitimate purpose other than cryptojacking the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
